vitals.context: POD#4 — after spontaneous conversion back to normal sinus rhythm (~2 hours after onset), without rate- or rhythm-control medication or cardioversion.
POD#4 — recorded during the 2-hour atrial fibrillation episode. Ventricular rate fluctuating 70-105; blood pressure unaffected; patient asymptomatic.
POD#4 AM (anticipated discharge next day) — baseline, sinus rhythm, afebrile, hemodynamically normal.
POD#3 evening — sinus throughout the day; no telemetry events.
POD#3 — no arrhythmia on telemetry. Tolerating diet, ambulating.
POD#2 — no arrhythmia on telemetry. Afebrile.
POD#1 evening — sinus; no further telemetry runs since the morning.

vitals.heartRhythm: Sinus with 3 brief self-limited AF runs (15-20 sec) on telemetry over preceding 24 h
vitals.context: POD#1 — clinically improving. Blood pressure and heart rate currently normal between runs; the AF runs were brief, asymptomatic, and self-terminating.
Immediate post-op (open cholecystectomy) — extubated, stable in PACU, on telemetry.
ED arrival — fevers, chills, RUQ/epigastric pain. Sinus tachycardia, febrile. CT obtained; broad-spectrum antibiotics started; to OR.
Most recent PCP visit (Dr. Feldman) — hypertension above goal on three agents; antihypertensive adherence reviewed.
PCP visit — hypertension poorly controlled; HCTZ dose reviewed. Flu shot.
PCP visit (months after husband's death) — grief discussed (PHQ-9 8); BP above goal.
